hero.tagline: The Next-Gen GPU & DSA Language Achieves 5x Productivity
hero.subtitle: Write less code. Catch more bugs. Ship faster GPU kernels.

easyToUse.title: Easy to Use
easyToUse.subtitle: Start with less, achieve more
easyToUse.desc1: Achieve extreme performance with zero-cost abstractions
easyToUse.desc2: The most intuitive GPU & DSA programming language among all competitors
easyToUse.desc3: 40% of equivalent CUDA code — higher abstraction than Triton
easyToUse.desc4: Work on tensors and tiles, not raw buffers and pointers
easyToUse.point1: Work on tensors rather than raw buffers
easyToUse.point2: Avoid the complexity of HW programming — TMA, DMA, tensor-core
easyToUse.point3: Make parallel programs easier to write and reason about
easyToUse.point4: Seamless C++ and Python integration — use CroqPy or link any C++ library

zeroCost.title: Zero-Cost Abstraction
zeroCost.subtitle: High-level code, vendor-matching speed
zeroCost.desc1: Match or exceed optimized vendor libraries with near-zero performance gap
zeroCost.desc2: No runtime overhead — static dispatch, no vtable, no interpreter
zeroCost.desc3: End-to-end LLM serving within 5% of native on Hopper
zeroCost.desc4: Compiles to the same optimal PTX as hand-tuned CUDA
zeroCost.e2eNote: Model-level throughput is slightly below native because certain corner-case shapes and attention fusions still require tuning. Kernel-level performance already matches or exceeds vendors — the remaining gap closes as we tune more operator variants.
zeroCost.whyBody: Provide easy-to-use, high-level abstractions while eliminating runtime cost as much as possible through compile-time optimizations. You write concise, readable code; the compiler resolves abstractions via static dispatch and shape specialization to produce near-optimal code.

safety.title: Compile-Time Safety
safety.subtitle: Catch bugs before they run
safety.desc1: 353 compile-time diagnostic checks across 7 compiler modules
safety.desc2: Catch tiling mismatches, shape errors, and DMA bugs before they run
safety.desc3: 1,319 runtime assertions guard every kernel in production
safety.desc4: Eliminate entire classes of bugs that plague CUDA development
safety.point1: Detect tiling mismatches at compile time
safety.point2: Enable aggressive runtime checks for easier debugging
safety.point3: Eliminate entire classes of DMA-related bugs

dynamic.title: Dynamic Shapes
dynamic.subtitle: Symbolic dimensions, zero boilerplate
dynamic.desc1: First-ever dynamic shared memory for GPU & DSA kernel programming
dynamic.desc2: Symbolic dimensions — write once, run on any shape
dynamic.desc3: Derived dimensions auto-computed: PACKED_K, META_COLS, grid, SMEM
dynamic.desc4: No template metaprogramming, no recompilation per shape
dynamic.point3: Static and runtime memory support unified

ai.title: Born for AI Tuning
ai.subtitle: Language meets machine intelligence
ai.desc1: ~500 tokens per kernel — highest source density among GPU DSLs, keeping entire kernels inside AI context windows
ai.desc2: Unified primitives (dma.copy, mma.row) hide error-prone boilerplate, reducing token overhead and AI confusion
ai.desc3: 353 compile-time + 1,319 runtime checks serve as AI guardrails — 200+ autonomous iterations without failure
ai.desc4: Structured, AI-readable compiler diagnostics and well-documented CLI for autonomous tuning agents
ai.desc5: AI achieved 1127 TFLOPS from 671 baseline (+67.9%) in a single session of 68 iterations
